title: Optimized Binary Search in Rotated Array

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input is an integer array a[] and a target number x. Array a[] is rotated a[0] times.
statement.outputFormat: Return 0 if x is not present in a[], otherwise return the index of the number in the array.
statement.notes: Array a[] is sorted in ascending order and rotated to the right by a[0] times.

examples.explanation: The input is an array a[] of size 5 with values 1, 3, 5, 2, 3. The array is rotated 1 time to the right. The target number is 2. The binary search algorithm should return the index 1.

hints.text: Consider using a modified binary search approach to optimize the search process.

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: Optimize the binary search by taking advantage of the rotation count. This can be achieved by finding the pivot element and adjusting the search space accordingly.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach would involve searching the entire array. However, this approach is inefficient for large arrays.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution can be achieved by using a modified binary search approach that takes into account the rotation count.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves finding the pivot element and adjusting the search space to optimize the binary search.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves demonstrating that the modified binary search approach is able to find the target number with a linear time complexity.
editorial.dryRun: Consider performing a dry run of the algorithm to understand how it works.
commonMistakes: Using a naive binary search approach without considering the rotation count

functionSignature.python: def search(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> int:
